entries.title: Instagram Platform — February 8, 2018
entries.description: Content Publishing API — Beta partners can now include hashtags when publishing photos via the /{ig-user-id}/media edge. #crazywildebeest FTW!

entries.title: Instagram Platform — February 22, 2018
entries.description: Public fields — The /{ig-user-id} , /{ig-comment-id} , and /{ig-media-id} nodes will now return all public fields when accessed through an edge via field expansion. Refer to each node’s reference document to see which fields are public.

entries.title: Instagram Platform — March 8, 2018
entries.description: Public fields — The timestamp field on the /{ig-media-id} node is now a public field and can be returned via field expansion.

entries.title: Instagram Platform — March 13, 2018
entries.description: Content Publishing API — Beta partners can now use the /{ig-user-id}/media edge to tag locations and public Instagram users when publishing photos.

entries.title: Instagram Platform — April 23, 2018
entries.description: Insights API — Insights will now include ad activity generated through the API, Facebook ads interfaces, and Instagram’s Promote feature. This affects the following metrics: impressions reach impressions reach

entries.title: Instagram Platform — April 24, 2018
entries.description: /{ig-comment-id} node: Added a new username field. For GET requests, the user field will not be included in responses unless the User making the request owns the Comment; instead, we will return username for all commenters. This also applies to queries on Comments made through other APIs, such as the Mentions API. Added a new username field. For GET requests, the user field will not be included in responses unless the User making the request owns the Comment; instead, we will return username...

entries.title: Instagram Platform — May 1, 2018
entries.description: Business Verification — In order to use the Instagram Graph API, all apps must undergo Business Verification , which is part of the App Review process and now required for all Instagram Graph API endpoints. Apps previously reviewed before May 1st, 2018, have to be reviewed again, and have until August 1st, 2018 to do so, or lose access to the API.

entries.title: Instagram Platform — June 7, 2018
entries.description: /{ig-media-id} node — You can now use field expansion to get the permalink field on media objects.

entries.title: Instagram Platform — October 23, 2018
entries.description: /{ig-media-id}/comments edge — GET requests made using API version 3.1 or older will have results returned in chronological order. Requests made using version 3.2+ will have results returned in reverse chronological order.

entries.title: Instagram Platform — October 31, 2018
entries.description: Hashtag Search API — You can now search for media tagged with specific hashtags by using our new Hashtag Search API . #spooky !

entries.title: Instagram Platform — May 9, 2019
entries.description: Webhooks — The story_insights field now requires the instagram_manage_insights permission instead of instagram_manage_comments .

entries.title: Instagram Platform — May 22, 2019
entries.description: Instagram Creator Accounts — The API now supports Instagram Creator Accounts ⁠ , with two exceptions. (1) The Content Publishing API cannot be used by Instagram Creators, and (2) the Business Discovery API can be used by Creators but can only target Businesses.

entries.title: Instagram Platform — August 13, 2019
entries.description: Business Discovery — The Business Discovery API can now be used to get data about other Instagram Creator accounts.

entries.title: Instagram Platform — December 3, 2019
entries.description: Insights — To align API behavior with Instagram app behavior, insights on IG Users are now only available on IG Users that have 100 or more followers.

entries.title: Instagram Platform — May 5, 2020
entries.description: Hashtag Search — This change applies to v7.0+ — You can now request the timestamp field on IG Media returned by GET /{ig-hashtag-id}/top_media and GET /{ig-hashtag-id}/recent_media Hashtag Search queries. For example: GET /{ig-hashtag-id}/top_media?fields=timestamp .

entries.title: Instagram Platform — November 10, 2020
entries.description: IG User Insights — The follower_count values now align more closely with their corresponding values displayed in the Instagram app. In addition, follower_count now returns a maximum of 30 days of data instead of 2 years. This change applies to v9.0+ and will apply to all versions May 9, 2021.

entries.title: Instagram Platform — December 2, 2020
entries.description: For Stories created by users in the EEA, the replies metric will now return a value of 0 . For Stories created by users outside the EEA, the replies metric will return the number of replies, but replies made my users in the EEA will not be included in its calculation.

entries.title: Instagram Platform — March 16, 2021
entries.description: March 16, 2021

entries.title: Instagram Platform — April 9, 2021
entries.description: The status field on an IG Container now returns an error subcode if the container’s error_code field value is ERROR . The IG Media Insights video_views metric now supports albums and will return the sum of video_views on all videos in the album instead of 0 .

entries.title: Instagram Platform — April 12, 2021
entries.description: April 12, 2021

entries.title: Instagram Platform — April 14, 2021
entries.description: For stories created by users in Japan, the replies metric will now return a value of 0 . For stories created by users outside Japan, the replies metric will return the number of replies, but replies made by users in Japan will not be included in the calculation.

entries.title: Instagram Platform — October 5, 2021
entries.description: the media_product_type field will return FEED instead of IGTV the video_title field will not be returned Instagram Webhooks comments and mentions fields are now supported

entries.title: Instagram Platform — October 20, 2021
entries.description: from — returns an object containing the IGSID ( id ) and username ( username ) of the comment creator. parent_id — returns the ID of the parent IG Comment if this comment was created on another IG Comment (i.e. a reply to another comment). from.id — IGSID of the Instagram user who created the comment. from.username — Username of the Instagram user who created the comment media.id — ID of the IG Media upon which the comment was made. media.media_product_type — Surface (published location) of t...

entries.title: Instagram Platform — November 9, 2021
entries.description: a new GET /ig-user/live_media edge can return live video IG Media being broadcast by your app user at the time of the request the media field on an IG Comment now returns and object containing both the ID ( id ) and published location ( media_product_type ) of the media upon which the comment was made a new live_comments Instagram Webhooks field can send notifications containing live comments made on your app users’ live videos as they are being broadcast

entries.title: Connect 2024: The responsible approach we’re taking to generative AI
entries.description: With the rapidly evolving AI landscape, we recognize the importance of sharing our responsibility and safety approach with everyone.

entries.title: Introducing the Digital Twin Catalog from Reality Labs Research
entries.description: Reality Labs Research is releasing the Digital Twin Catalog (DTC), which we believe is the world’s largest and highest quality 3D object model dataset for 3D reconstruction research.

entries.title: Making data science more accessible to enterprises with Llama
entries.description: By leveraging Llama 3.1, MetaLearner enables enterprises to tap into powerful AI capabilities without the need for specialized expertise, making advanced analytics accessible to non-technical users.

entries.title: Instagram Platform — October 3, 2024
entries.description: October 3, 2024

entries.title: How Meta Movie Gen could usher in a new AI-enabled era for content creators
entries.description: Today, we’re excited to premiere Meta Movie Gen, our breakthrough generative AI research for media, which includes modalities like image, video, and audio.

entries.title: October 8, 2024
entries.description: Added Flow name referencing in API, Flow JSON support in Message Templates API, and dedicated endpoint setup in Builder.

entries.title: How Neuromnia is transforming ABA therapy with Llama 3.1
entries.description: Harnessing Llama 3.1, Neuromnia recently developed Nia, a human-centric AI co-pilot for Applied Behavior Analysis therapy.

entries.title: How Llama helped CodeGPT become one of the top AI-powered coding assistants
entries.description: CodeGPT integrates large language models like Llama to make developers and CTOs more productive.

entries.title: Partnering with Blumhouse, creators, and the entertainment industry as we develop Meta Movie Gen
entries.description: We’re sharing initial results from our work with award-winning production company Blumhouse and select creators—part of a pilot program focused on creative industry feedback.

entries.title: Sharing new research, models, and datasets from Meta FAIR
entries.description: Today, Meta FAIR is releasing several new research artifacts in support of our goal of achieving advanced machine intelligence (AMI) while also supporting open science and reproducibility.

entries.title: How an online gifting site is using Llama to help protect customer privacy
entries.description: Untukmu.AI, a platform that offers personalized gift recommendations, designed a semi-decentralized personal assistant that ensures the company won’t have access to customer data.

entries.title: Roboflow estimates a 74-year time savings across its community from using Meta’s Segment Anything
entries.description: As a company with a mission to help make the world more programmable, Roboflow uses SAM to help build systems that enable their customers to have visual understanding for just about anything.

entries.title: Introducing quantized Llama models with increased speed and a reduced memory footprint
entries.description: As our first quantized models in this Llama category, these instruction-tuned models retain the quality and safety of the original 1B and 3B models, while achieving 2-4x speedup.

entries.title: Advancing embodied AI through progress in touch perception, dexterity, and human-robot interaction
entries.description: Meta FAIR is publicly releasing several new research artifacts that advance robotics and support our goal of reaching advanced machine intelligence (AMI).

entries.title: Highlights from the first-ever Llama hackathon in India
entries.description: Teams embarked on a 30-hour journey to create AI solutions tackling real-world challenges, leveraging Meta’s Llama 3 models and WhatsApp APIs.

entries.title: November 12, 2024
entries.description: Released Flow JSON versions 6.0 and 6.1 with CalendarPicker component, nested expressions, update_data action, and published Flow editing.

entries.title: IBM and Llama: Working to enable AI builder creativity globally
entries.description: IBM and Meta are implementing the combined power of IBM’s watsonx AI and data platform and Llama to help businesses reach their AI goals.

entries.title: Open Catalyst experiments 2024
entries.description: Meta FAIR is releasing a large-scale dataset of experimental results on various materials, providing valuable insights for the development of new catalysts.

entries.title: Building a chatbot based on Llama to engage an intergovernmental organization’s stakeholders
entries.description: Accenture turned to Llama for a leading global intergovernmental body’s first large-scale, public-facing generative AI application.

entries.title: Introducing SPDL: Faster AI model training with thread-based data loading
entries.description: SPDL is a framework-agnostic data loading solution that uses multi-threading, which achieves high-throughput in a regular Python interpreter (built without free-threading option enabled).

entries.title: Extending healthcare access to underserved communities with AI
entries.description: The team behind BiMediX2, an Arabic-English medical large multimodal model, aims to expand healthcare access in Africa and the Middle East.

entries.title: Scaling semiconductor expertise with Llama-powered Domain-Expert Agents
entries.description: Aitomatic enables semiconductor companies to build Domain-Expert Agents to capture and scale their deep domain expertise using Llama-based foundation models.

entries.title: Instagram Platform — December 4, 2024
entries.description: December 4, 2024

entries.title: Advancing Neuromotor Interfaces by Open Sourcing Surface Electromyography (sEMG) Datasets for Pose Estimation and Surface Typing
entries.description: We’re releasing emg2qwerty and emg2pose—two large datasets and benchmarks for sEMG-based typing and pose estimation, as part of the NeurIPS 2024 Datasets and Benchmarks track.

entries.title: How Inarix is using DINOv2 to revolutionize the agricultural supply chain
entries.description: Inarix is transforming the agricultural industry by turning smartphones into pocket laboratories using Meta FAIR’s DinoV2.

entries.title: December 10, 2024
entries.description: Released Flow JSON version 6.2 with NavigationList component, regex validation for TextInput, and Flow migration API.

entries.title: Sharing new research, models, and datasets from Meta FAIR
entries.description: Meta FAIR is releasing new research artifacts that highlight our recent innovations in developing agents, robustness and safety, and architectures that facilitate machine learning.

entries.title: How Spotify is using Llama to create personalized recommendations and enhance content discovery
entries.description: Spotify uses Llama to deliver contextualized recommendations to boost artist discovery and create an even richer user experience.

entries.title: The future of AI: Built with Llama
entries.description: As we close out 2024, Meta is leading the industry forward in AI product and technology experiences and setting a new standard for how the industry builds and advances AI.

entries.title: Introducing Nymeria, a dataset for improving human motion prediction for AR and VR devices
entries.description: The Nymeria dataset provides egocentric human motion in the wild at an unprecedented scale, capturing a broad spectrum of people engaging in everyday activities across varied locations.

entries.title: Finding the perfect book to read next with a Llama-based assistant
entries.description: Scribd, Inc.’s Everand reading service is home to a global library of millions of ebooks, audiobooks and more.

entries.title: How TU Dresden is advancing precision oncology and transforming healthcare AI
entries.description: TUD Dresden University of Technology’s Clinical AI research group is transforming cancer care through computational innovation, focusing on precision oncology.

entries.title: How Virgo is using DINOv2 to analyze endoscopy videos for precision medicine
entries.description: Virgo, a company based in San Diego, California, believes endoscopy video and AI are a powerful combination that could lead to improved patient outcomes.

entries.title: How Llama is helping Saama deliver new possibilities in personalized medicine and data-driven care
entries.description: OpenBioLLM, a series of fine-tuned Llama models from life sciences company Saama, streamlines tasks that can accelerate clinical trials and potentially create new possibilities in personalized medicine.

entries.title: January 14, 2025
entries.description: Released Flow JSON version 6.3 with Chips Selector component, RichText footer support, and simplified Flow APIs.

entries.title: How digital artist Josephine Miller uses Meta Segment Anything to help design the future of fashion
entries.description: Josephine, a London-based XR creative designer and content creator, takes us behind the scenes of how she uses open source technology in her work.

entries.title: Scaling an AI coding assistant built with Llama to hundreds of thousands of users
entries.description: Codeium’s AI-enabled coding assistant leverages Llama for its free and premiere offerings.

entries.title: How BrightHeart uses Meta’s DINOv2 to transform fetal heart screenings
entries.description: BrightHeart has created an AI-powered medical software with the support of Meta’s DINOv2 model to help clinicians identify or rule out signs suggestive of congenital heart defects.

entries.title: How Altana employs Llama to elevate global value chain management
entries.description: Altana uses AI to help businesses and governments manage their global supply chains, providing insight into extended supplier and distribution networks.

entries.title: Enlisting Llama in India’s first open source audio language model
entries.description: Sarvam used Llama to develop enterprise voice AI agents with improved reasoning capabilities that are proficient in 10 Indian languages.

entries.title: Using AI to decode language from the brain and advance our understanding of human communication
entries.description: Today, in collaboration with the Basque Center on Cognition, Brain and Language, we’re excited to share two breakthroughs that show how AI can help advance our understanding of human intelligence, leading us closer to AMI.

entries.title: Advancing machine intelligence through human-centered research
entries.description: Meta FAIR is sharing some of our most recent research and models that support our goal of achieving AMI, and our long-held commitment to sharing open and reproducible science.

entries.title: AI Artifacts: An interview with Ruben Fro and Benjamin Bardou
entries.description: In honor of this year’s AI Action Summit, we partnered with the Bibliothèque Nationale de France (BnF), Fisheye Immersive, Convergence, and Institut Montaigne to commission two open source AI-generated art installations by innovative artists, Ruben Fro and Benjamin Bardou.

entries.title: Update: Expanding access to Meta Segment Anything 2.1 on Amazon SageMaker JumpStart
entries.description: Starting today, SAM 2.1 is available in Amazon SageMaker JumpStart, making it easier than ever to deploy SAM 2.1 and integrate it into new applications and workflows.

entries.title: Reimagining K-12 Education: How Blended Labs is raising the bar with AI-driven schools
entries.description: Blended, an educational technology and AI company based in Germany, is using Llama 3.1 as the foundation of its flagship product, Blended OS, an AI-native school operating system.

entries.title: EgoMimic: Georgia Tech PhD student uses Project Aria Research Glasses to help train humanoid robots
entries.description: Today, we’re highlighting new research from Georgia Tech that helps train robots to perform basic everyday tasks using egocentric recordings from wearers of Meta’s Project Aria research glasses.

entries.title: Dramatically accelerating patient-matching in clinical trials with open source
entries.description: Mendel AI’s flagship product, Mendel Hypercube, tackles clinical tasks. With Llama, Hypercube can also be used for trial matching and putting together patient groups.

entries.title: How Orakl Oncology is using DINOv2 to accelerate cancer treatment discovery
entries.description: Orakl Oncology aims to accelerate cancer research and drug development by combining experimental, lab-based insights and machine learning.

entries.title: How Sevilla FC is discovering future soccer stars with Llama
entries.description: Sevilla FC’s data department partnered with IBM to create Scout Advisor, a generative AI-driven scouting tool designed and deployed on watsonx, with Llama 3.1 70B Instruct.

entries.title: How Sofya is taking clinical reasoning to the next level with Llama
entries.description: Sofya turned to Llama with the goal of fostering the development of healthcare solutions in Brazil and Latin America.

entries.title: Building foundation models for understanding human pathology using DINOv2
entries.description: When Dr. Faisal Mahmood set up his laboratory at the intersection of pathology and machine learning. The team credits open source models, including Meta’s DINO, with helping them do their work.

entries.title: March 11, 2025
entries.description: Released Flow JSON version 7.0 with large label variant, on-select-action behavior reversion, and form validation improvements.

entries.title: How Smartly is improving customer service and automating technical support with generative AI
entries.description: Smartly, a digital advertising platform, harnesses the power of AI to scale ads across channels.

entries.title: Meta at SXSW: How Llama and open source AI is leveling the playing field and enabling innovation
entries.description: At SXSW, Meta and Llama developers showcased the impact of open source AI, and participated in policy discussions and hands-on demos.

entries.title: Our open source Llama models are helping to spur economic growth in the US
entries.description: We’ve shared how Llama’s impact has already been felt by businesses and entrepreneurs. And today, we’re looking at how Llama is helping to spur economic growth in the US.

entries.title: Instagram Platform — March 24, 2025
entries.description: Alternative text for image posts

entries.title: How Cornerstone is transforming XR training to build essential skills for the modern workforce
entries.description: Cornerstone is transforming how employees learn. Using Llama, they’re taking that experience to the next level through extended reality training scenarios.

entries.title: How Tavus is helping to make AI videos feel like real conversations
entries.description: Tavus, an AI video research company, leverages advanced AI models to create human-like digital interactions that feel as authentic as real human conversations.

entries.title: The Llama 4 herd: The beginning of a new era of natively multimodal AI innovation
entries.description: We’re introducing Llama 4 Scout and Llama 4 Maverick, the first open-weight natively multimodal models with unprecedented context support and our first built using a mixture-of-experts (MoE) architecture.

entries.title: Instagram Platform — April 8, 2025
entries.description: Meta oEmbed Read Feature Apps created after April 8, 2025 that implement oEmbed will use the new Meta oEmbed Read feature. Existing apps that already use the current oEmbed Read feature will be automatically updated to the new Meta oEmbed Read feature by November 3, 2025. oEmbed Response Updates

entries.title: April 8, 2025
entries.description: Froze Flow JSON versions 2.1 through 4.0 and added support for Flows with other buttons in message templates.

entries.title: Advancing AI systems through progress in perception, localization, and reasoning
entries.description: Meta FAIR is releasing several new research artifacts that advance our understanding of perception and support our goal of achieving advanced machine intelligence (AMI).

entries.title: How Litmos is using Llama to make corporate learning more intuitive
entries.description: With Llama 3.1 8B, Litmos created an AI-powered assistant that enables natural language search, making the LMS more intuitive and user-friendly.

entries.title: Everything we announced at our first-ever LlamaCon
entries.description: Here’s a look at what we announced at LlamaCon and how you can get started with our newest releases.

entries.title: Sharing new open source protection tools and advancements in AI privacy and security
entries.description: Today, we’re releasing new Llama protection tools for the open source AI community.

entries.title: How Meta Segment Anything Model enables Cutouts in the Instagram Edits app
entries.description: People can use Cutouts to edit across several layers of video, applying filters to specific parts of videos and easily place elements like text and stickers behind objects.

entries.title: How Common Sense Machines uses Meta Segment Anything Model and AI to generate production-ready 3D assets
entries.description: Common Sense Machines uses Meta Segment Anything Model 2 to analyze 2D images and video and translate their components to 3D.

entries.title: Meet the winners of our first-ever LlamaCon Hackathon
entries.description: The challenge was to create a demonstrable project using the Llama API, Llama 4 Scout, or Llama 4 Maverick—or any combination of these cutting-edge tools—within just 24 hours.

entries.title: Sharing new breakthroughs and artifacts supporting molecular property prediction, language processing, and neuroscience
entries.description: Meta FAIR is sharing new research artifacts that highlight our commitment to advanced machine intelligence (AMI) through focused scientific and academic progress.

entries.title: Introducing the Llama Startup Program
entries.description: We’re excited to announce the Llama Startup Program, a new initiative to empower early stage startups to innovate and build generative AI applications with Llama.

entries.title: Inside Aria Gen 2: Explore the cutting-edge tech behind the device
entries.description: Today, we’re excited to share more about the technology inside Aria Gen 2. This includes an in-depth overview of the form factor, audio capabilities, battery life, upgraded cameras and sensors, on-device compute, and more.

entries.title: June 10, 2025
entries.description: Released Flow JSON version 7.1 with updated ChipsSelector component and new Image Carousel component.

entries.title: Introducing the V-JEPA 2 world model and new benchmarks for physical reasoning
entries.description: We’re excited to share V-JEPA 2, the first world model trained on video that enables state-of-the-art understanding and prediction, as well as zero-shot planning and robot control in new environments.

entries.description: Business Login for Instagram enable_fb_login force_authentication

entries.title: Instagram Platform — June 16, 2025
Announcing the inaugural Llama Startup Program cohort
entries.description: At Meta, we believe in the potential of early-stage startups to drive innovation in the generative AI market, and through the Llama Startup Program, we aim to lower the barrier to entry for getting started with Llama models.

entries.title: How Llama helps drive engineering efficiency at a major Australian bank
entries.description: ANZ, one of Australia's Big Four banks, is driving engineering efficiency with Llama.

entries.title: Modeling natural conversational dynamics with Seamless Interaction
entries.description: Modeling natural conversational dynamics with Seamless Interaction

entries.description: Released Flow JSON version 7.2 with improved validation for nested expressions in Flow JSON actions.

entries.title: Joining forces with AWS on a new program to help startups build with Llama
entries.description: We're joining forces with Amazon Web Services to announce a new program that will provide resources and support to 30 promising startups in the U.S. that are building with Llama.

entries.title: How Upwork helps freelancers win more work with Llama

entries.title: How Llama helps Biofy Technologies in the fight against antibiotic resistance

entries.title: Escaping the Fork: How Meta Modernized WebRTC Across 50+ Use Cases
entries.description: At Meta, WebRTC powers real-time audio and video across various platforms. But forking a large open-source project like WebRTC within our monorepo presents unique challenges – over time, an internal fork can drift behind upstream, cutting itself off from community upgrades. We’re sharing how we escaped this “forking trap” – from building a dual-stack architecture [...] Read More... The post Escaping the Fork: How Meta Modernized WebRTC Across 50+ Use Cases appeared first on Engineering at Meta .

entries.title: Post-Quantum Cryptography Migration at Meta: Framework, Lessons, and Takeaways
entries.description: We’re sharing lessons learned from Meta’s post-quantum cryptography (PQC) migration to help other organizations strengthen their resilience as industry transitions to post-quantum cryptography standards. We’re proposing the idea of PQC Migration Levels to help teams within organizations manage the complexity of PQC migration for their various use cases. By outlining Meta’s approach to this work [...] Read More... The post Post-Quantum Cryptography Migration at Meta: Framework, Lessons, and Ta...

By leveraging encoded domain expertise across a unified, standardized tool interface these agents help save power and free up engineers’ time away from addressing performance issues to innovating on [...] Read More... The post Capacity Efficiency at Meta: How Unified AI Agents Optimize Performance at Hyp...

Under this new framework, we’ve made tangible improvements [...] Read More... The post Modernizing the Facebook Groups Search to Unlock the...

entries.title: How Meta Is Strengthening End-to-End Encrypted Backups
entries.description: The HSM-based Backup Key Vault Meta’s HSM-based Backup Key Vault provides the foundation for end-to-end encrypted backups for WhatsApp and Messenger. The system allows people to protect their backed-up message history with a recovery code, ensuring that the recovery code is stored in tamper-resistant hardware security modules (HSMs) and is inaccessible to Meta, cloud storage [...] Read More... The post How Meta Is Strengthening End-to-End Encrypted Backups appeared first on Engineering at Meta .

entries.title: Instagram Platform — May 6, 2026
Labyrinth 1.1: Making End-to-End Encrypted Backups Even More Reliable
entries.description: We’re rolling out version 1.1 of Labyrinth, the encrypted storage system and protocol that secures messages and history on Messenger. Labyrinth 1.1 enhances the reliability of end-to-end encrypted backups with a new sub-protocol that helps messages survive the loss of a device, a switched device, and long gaps between sign-ins. Read our updated white paper, [...] Read More... The post Labyrinth 1.1: Making End-to-End Encrypted Backups Even More Reliable appeared first on Engineering at Meta .

entries.description: Child exploitation is a horrific crime and every day, we work aggressively to fight this kind of abuse both on and off our platforms. And we want to be clear: we take these concerns seriously, we never want this content on our platforms, and we’re committed to improving our efforts to combat it. entries.title: Modernizing the Meta Ads Service With an Open-Source Kernel Scheduler
entries.description: TL; DR At Meta’s scale, a few milliseconds of latency degradation can have a significant negative impact on ads performance.
